title: Doc-Init Rec#6 — docs/initialization.md + README update
description: Write docs/initialization.md documenting the complete 7-Stage user journey from running fab init through completing the agents-md-init skill interaction. Include the 4-scenario degradation table (Claude Code in-session / cross-session / external terminal / CI/non-AI). Update README.md to reference docs/initialization.md and preview the initialization flow. Both documents should be written from the perspective of a developer who just ran fab init and wants to know what to do next.

files.target: 7-Stage user journey for fab init + agents-md-init skill
files.change: New doc: Stage 1 (fab init evidence collection + .fabric/ write), Stage 2 (stdout reason text), Stage 3 (agents-md-init skill auto-triggers), Stage 4 (Phase 1 framework confirmation interview), Stage 5 (Phase 2 invariants elicitation), Stage 6 (Phase 3 AGENTS.md construction + init-context.json write), Stage 7 (ongoing maintenance with agents-md skill); include 4-scenario degradation table (Claude Code in-session / cross-session Stop hook / external terminal fab init / CI non-AI environment); include FAQ: 'I ran fab init but no skill triggered — what now?'

files.target: Add initialization flow section + reference to docs/initialization.md
files.change: Add '## Getting Started' or '## Initializing a Project' section (≤15 lines) describing: run fab init → follow agents-md-init skill → AGENTS.md generated; link to docs/initialization.md for full 7-Stage details; preserve all existing README content unchanged

implementation: Write docs/initialization.md following the 7-Stage lifecycle from ANL-fab-doc-init-werewolf discussion.md Round 4 narrative; draw Stage 3-6 from the SKILL.md Phase 1/2/3 content created in TASK-003
Write the 4-scenario degradation table: column headers (Scenario | Trigger mechanism | Result); rows for each of the 4 scenarios from conclusions.json key_conclusions point 4
Add FAQ section answering: 'I ran fab init outside Claude Code' and 'The skill didn't trigger — how to manually invoke'
Update README.md: insert ≤15-line section referencing the new doc; ensure existing README sections remain unchanged

reference.examples: Stage descriptions from discussion.md Round 4 section; 4-scenario matrix from conclusions.json key_conclusions[3]

criteria: docs/initialization.md exists and contains all 7 Stage headings
docs/initialization.md contains the 4-scenario degradation table with all 4 scenarios
README.md references docs/initialization.md via a markdown link
After reading docs/initialization.md, a developer can answer: 'I ran fab init — what is my next step?'
README.md existing content is preserved (no deletions from prior sections)

rationale.chosen_approach: Separate docs/initialization.md keeps README concise while providing full depth for developers who need it
alternatives_considered: Embed everything in README (makes README very long)
decision_factors: Developer experience: clear 'what do I do next' answer
Separation of concerns: README = overview, docs/ = depth
rationale.tradeoffs: Requires developer to navigate to docs/ for full details; README link mitigates discoverability

manual_checks: Read docs/initialization.md — verify all 7 stages present and clearly described
Read the 4-scenario table — verify all 4 scenarios have trigger mechanism and result columns
Read README.md — verify new section present and link to docs/initialization.md works
Run markdown linter on docs/initialization.md if configured in project
success_metrics: docs/initialization.md: 7 stages present, 4-scenario table present, FAQ section present
README.md: link to docs/initialization.md present, no existing content removed

risks.description: Documentation becomes stale if Stage 3-6 SKILL.md content changes in TASK-003
risks.mitigation: Write docs at high level of abstraction (reference skill phases, not exact prompts); note that SKILL.md is the authoritative source for interview questions
risks.fallback: If SKILL.md changes after doc is written, update docs/initialization.md as a follow-up
